First dose administered 2026-03-27. |\n\n## Quick PK Assessment\n- Current dosing parameters are present and internally consistent in the latest config.\n- Patient-specific PK clearance values range from **2.8 to 4.1 mL/min**.\n- Based on this config alone, there are **no obvious dosing inconsistencies or instability flags**.\n- A full assessment of live PK model stability would require model/runtime validation beyond the dosing file.\n", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and saved a quick report for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary:

- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Source:** `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`
- **Last updated:** 2026-03-28

| Patient | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|
| CU-001 | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min |
| CU-002 | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min |
| CU-003 | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min |

Clinical notes:
- **CU-001:** Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan
- **CU-002:** Responding well; weight gain improving
- **CU-003:** New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27

On PK model stability: based on the **current dosing config**, everything looks internally consistent and I don’t see any obvious red flags in the listed PK parameters. That said, this file alone doesn’t confirm full live model/runtime stability — only that the latest stored dosing parameters look sound.

If you want, I can also format this into a shorter email-ready note for Dr. Martinez.
